claim: For all integers n >= 1, sigma(n) * phi(n) is at most n^2.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is prime, then sigma(n) * phi(n) equals n^2.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then sigma(n) * phi(n) equals n^2.
For all integers n >= 1, if n is a prime power, then sigma(n) * phi(n) is at most n^2.
